# The Run Rate

> Growth intelligence for fitness, wellness and health tech. Data-led analysis, growth strategy and market intelligence for fitness studio operators, gym chains, wellness brands and health tech companies — not generic marketing advice.

## About
- [About](https://therunrate.co/about/): Who The Run Rate is and what it covers
- [Work With Us](https://therunrate.co/work-with-us/): Advertising and sponsorship
- [Newsletter](https://newsletter.therunrate.co/subscribe): Weekly Roundup (free) + The Take (paid) + Growth Playbooks

## Studio Growth
- [Gen Z Didn't Just Quit Drinking. They Found Something Better.](https://therunrate.co/studio-growth/gen-z-fitness-spending-sober-curious-studios/): US drinking hit its lowest rate since 1939 while Gen Z fitness spend rose 9%. This isn't sobriety — it's a full reallocation of where a generation spends its social currency.
- [3 in 4 Instructors Feel Underpaid. Paying Everyone The Same Will Not Fix It.](https://therunrate.co/studio-growth/instructor-pay-bifurcation-fitness-studios/): New instructor data shows a decade of flat pay. But the real fix isn't a blanket raise — it's admitting some instructors are talent and most are commodity, and building a pay structure that says so.
- [A Free Rest Club Went Viral in NYC. What Smart Studios Can Learn.](https://therunrate.co/studio-growth/club-rest-stop-fitness-studio-acquisition-funnel/): Club Rest Stop charges nothing and still can't keep up with demand. For fitness studios chasing member acquisition, the lesson isn't to sell rest — it's to give away community the same way, and build the paid layer behind it.
- [AI Can Write Your Workout Plan But It Can't Show Up For You.](https://therunrate.co/studio-growth/ai-workout-plans-accountability-fitness-studios/): Generic AI programming is now free. The thing it can't replicate — a human who shows up and holds you to it — is what studios are actually selling.
- [Equinox Doesn't Compete on Price. It Competes on Identity.](https://therunrate.co/studio-growth/equinox-identity-pricing-playbook/): Equinox raised its membership price — again — while boutique studios are racing to the ClassPass floor. The difference isn't the gym. It's the identity contract. Here's the pricing playbook most operators are ignoring.
- [ClassPass Trained Consumers to Expect Optionality. Now Studios Are Paying for It.](https://therunrate.co/industry/classpass-optionality-consumer-behavior/): ClassPass didn't just build a discovery platform — it rewired how fitness consumers think about commitment. Studios that understand the optionality mindset (and build against it) are retaining members. The ones that ignore it are filling their classes at 40% margin.
- [Pilates Isn't a Workout Anymore. It's a Business Model.](https://therunrate.co/studio-growth/pilates-business-model-boutique-fitness/): Pilates studios that named their method, built an in-group, and priced above the ClassPass floor are quietly outperforming everyone else. The playbook isn't complicated — most studios just haven't committed to it.
- [Strength Training Just Replaced Weight Loss as the Top Gym Goal. Is Your Class Mix Ready?](https://therunrate.co/studio-growth/strength-training-replacing-weight-loss-gym-goal/): Trainers are reporting the same shift across the board: members aren't walking in to lose weight anymore. They're walking in to get strong. The studios that retool their programming and messaging first will own the next five years of member acquisition.
- [57% of Fitness Studio Members Churn in Year One. Most Operators Don't Know Why.](https://therunrate.co/studio-growth/fitness-studio-member-churn-year-one/): The data is damning — and it's not about price, location, or equipment. Members who leave tell a consistent story. The question is whether you're listening before they go.
- [80% of New Members Come From 3 Sources. Most Studios Are Funding the Other 20.](https://therunrate.co/studio-growth/fitness-studio-new-member-acquisition-sources/): Acquisition budgets get spread thin across every channel just in case. The data shows most studios are over-investing in low-yield channels while the three that actually drive new members stay underfunded.
- [You Think You Know Where Your Members Come From. You Don't.](https://therunrate.co/studio-growth/fitness-studio-member-attribution/): Most fitness studios credit referrals and Instagram — but have no data to back it up. The attribution gap costs more than most operators realize, and fixing it starts with three questions on a signup form.

## Tech & AI
- [Crunch Just Put Their Money on Pilates. But Can It Buy the Experience?](https://therunrate.co/tech-ai/pilates-connected-tech-boutique-experience-crunch/): Crunch bet big on 20 Pilates locations and connected reformer tech. The 66% surge in ClassPass Pilates bookings proves the workout scales — the question is whether the experience does too.
- [Apple Is Coming for Oura's Ring. Here's Why Your Members Should Care.](https://therunrate.co/tech-ai/apple-smart-ring-oura-wearables-war-fitness-studios/): Oura charges $6 a month on top of the hardware. If Apple folds the same recovery data into the Health app for free, the smart ring business model breaks overnight — and your members' wearable data gets a lot more interesting.
- [WHOOP Built a $1B Category. Now Every Boardroom Wants a Piece.](https://therunrate.co/tech-ai/whoop-billion-dollar-category-screenless-wearables-fitness-studios/): WHOOP proved you can sell a screenless rubber band for $239 a year and build a $10 billion company. Google, Garmin, and Apple are all copying the model. Here's what it means for fitness studios.
- [Connected Fitness Is Plan B. Here's What Plan A Looks Like.](https://therunrate.co/tech-ai/peloton-connected-fitness-plan-b-strength-longevity/): Peloton lost 218,000 subscribers in a year. It's not a product problem — it's a fitness goal problem. The studios winning right now are built around something screens can't sell.
- [Your Gym Software Just Became Your Distribution Channel](https://therunrate.co/tech-ai/octiv-explore-gym-software-owned-distribution-aggregators/): Octiv just launched a consumer discovery engine for 1,400 gyms across 40 countries — and it signals the end of paying aggregators to find your own customers.
- [Whoop Just Won a Fight Apple and Samsung Couldn't](https://therunrate.co/tech-ai/whoop-fda-blood-pressure-wearables-fitness-studios/): The FDA cleared Whoop's blood pressure feature — without the clearance Apple Watch has been chasing for years. Here's what it means for your studio.
- [Daxko Just Bought an API Stack. Here's Why That Matters More Than the Acquisition.](https://therunrate.co/tech-ai/daxko-acquires-fitnessforce-fitness-software-consolidation/): Most fitness software acquisitions lead with customer count. Daxko led with architecture. Here's what it means for operators on legacy platforms.
- [Planet Fitness Just Deployed AI Coaches. Here's What It Means for Independent Studios.](https://therunrate.co/tech-ai/planet-fitness-ai-coaches/): When the 2,500-location chain moves, the whole market shifts. Independent operators have a narrow window to counter-position before AI coaching becomes the baseline.

## Wellness
- [The Rise of Rest and Recovery as Fitness's Next Big Monetization Play.](https://therunrate.co/wellness/recovery-monetization-fitness-studios/): Wearables have made nervous-system data mainstream, and recovery studios are already proving people will pay for it — at wildly different price points. The trick is knowing which layer to sell, and what to give away first.
- [The Longevity Coach Is the New Personal Trainer. Fitness Studios Need to Decide Where They Stand.](https://therunrate.co/wellness/longevity-coach-trainer-fitness-studios/): NASM just launched a longevity coaching certification. That's not a credential — it's a category signal. The trainer who can speak to VO2 max, zone 2 cardio, and muscle mass preservation is now competing directly with your membership model.
- [Stop Checking Your Stats. Come Work Out.](https://therunrate.co/wellness/wearable-anxiety-health-trackers-fitness-studios/): WHOOP data now shows exactly when your members' stress peaks. The LA Times says health trackers are making people more anxious, not healthier. Your studio is the actual answer to both — most operators just haven't figured out how to say it.
- [The Longevity Clinic Is Coming for Your Members. Here's How to Not Lose Them.](https://therunrate.co/wellness/next-health-longevity-clinics-boutique-fitness/): Next Health is nearing 20 locations. When health optimization becomes infrastructure, boutique fitness studios either plug in or get bypassed.
- [Exercise Is the Best Dementia Prevention Tool We Have. Fitness Studios That Miss This Miss a Generation.](https://therunrate.co/wellness/exercise-dementia-longevity-fitness-studios/): New research confirms regular exercise measurably slows cognitive decline. The opportunity for operators isn't just a wellness positioning shift — it's a referral network, a new pricing tier, and a program design question your competitors haven't asked yet.
- [Apple Is Building a Women's Health OS. Your Gym Is Either a Node in It or Invisible.](https://therunrate.co/wellness/apple-health-ios27-womens-fitness/): iOS 27's perimenopause tracking and Apple Health+ aren't just features. They're Apple staking a claim on the 40+ female health journey — the same journey your millennial members are now on.

## Brands
- [Strava's IPO Isn't About Data. It's About Deeply Knowing Their Customer.](https://therunrate.co/brands/strava-ipo-know-your-customer-fitness-studios/): Strava filed for IPO the same week its niche clubs quadrupled — proof that knowing your customer's specific, obsessive interest beats trying to serve everyone. Boutique studios have the same advantage sitting in their client data.
- [The New Country Club Looks a Lot Like a Boutique Wellness Studio.](https://therunrate.co/brands/virgin-active-social-wellness-club-boutique-playbook/): Virgin Active's new Mayfair club blends fitness, recovery and lounge space into one membership — the exact community-first model boutique studios have used to win premium members for a decade.
- [Nike Had the Budget. It Never Had the Fitness Studio Moat.](https://therunrate.co/brands/nike-fitness-studios-shutdown-boutique-lessons/): Nike just closed its fitness studios. The failure wasn't a lack of capital — it was the absence of a method, a founder story, and a following that believed in either.
- [Crunch Has a New CEO. Here's What It Signals for the Budget Fitness Wars.](https://therunrate.co/brands/crunch-fitness-chequan-lewis-ceo-boutique-studio-competition/): Chequan Lewis just took the top job at Crunch Fitness. Leadership changes at 400-location chains don't happen quietly — and the direction he takes Crunch will tell boutique studio operators a lot about where the competitive pressure is heading next.
- [Lululemon Built a $10B Brand Without Ever Running a Single Ad. Here's What Fitness Studios Can Learn.](https://therunrate.co/brands/lululemon-brand-playbook-boutique-fitness-studios/): No TV. No print. No paid social. Just obsessive product focus and local community ambassadors. The playbook is free — and most studios aren't using it.
- [The Vibe Era Is Over. What Comes Next for Pilates, Boxing, and Barre.](https://therunrate.co/brands/pilates-boxing-barre-results-programming/): Format fitness built an industry on atmosphere, community, and the feeling a class gives you. The studios that thrive in the next decade will be the ones with the clearest answer to a harder question: what results did your members get?
